behaviors_invented.name: parseCsv
behaviors_invented.purpose: Minimal CSV parser used by renderBarChart auto-wiring.

behaviors_invented.name: renderBarChart
behaviors_invented.purpose: Inline SVG horizontal bar chart. Reads CSV/JSON from a script tag and renders into a [data-chart] SVG placeholder. Paper-warmed palette by default.

behaviors_invented.name: clickToExplain
behaviors_invented.purpose: Generic click-to-reveal harness for diagrams. Generalizes the flow-node pattern so future docs (arch diagrams, decision walks) can share the wiring.

behaviors_invented.name: glossary
behaviors_invented.purpose: Walks text nodes in scope, wraps each defined term in a focusable span, attaches click-to-reveal popover. Auto-wired from <script id="glossary"> JSON tag.

components_extended.name: system-note
components_extended.shape: single-class utility for callouts that follow a roadmap or decision-list
components_extended.purpose: Adds 28px top margin so a closing callout doesn't crowd the section above. Carried over from the first render.

components_extended.name: pillars + pillar
components_extended.purpose: Visual home for the four pillars. Mirrors .principles structure but tuned to four co-equal cards.

components_extended.name: chart-wrap + chart-header + chart-svg + chart-caption
components_extended.shape: boxed chart container with header, SVG, and small caption
components_extended.purpose: Frame for any inline chart rendered by runtime.js.

components_extended.name: term + term-tooltip
components_extended.shape: inline span with dashed underline + absolute-positioned popover
components_extended.purpose: Glossary term highlight and click-to-reveal definition popover.

pillar_audit.interactive: 8 — scroll-spy nav, focus mode, print, flow click-to-explain, decision-list details, contenteditable hero/lede/thesis/pullquote, glossary popovers. Eight live affordances.
pillar_audit.computational: 6 — runtime.js parses inline CSV and renders a live SVG bar chart of pillar scores; glossary walks the DOM and wires popovers dynamically. Not yet: decision walks, timelines, runnable code.
pillar_audit.aesthetic: 9 — warm-notebook palette intact, typography hierarchy clean, chart and glossary additions stay inside the theme's color and type system.
pillar_audit.warm: 9 — paper tones, serif display, gentle interaction, no chrome-junk. Inviting to reopen.

pullquotes: If you will re-open it, render it. If not, leave it markdown.

component_repurposes.repurposed_for: Three-layer system diagram (Layer 1 / Layer 2 / Layer 3) instead of V0/V1/V2 phases.
component_repurposes.v1_question: Should 'layer-grid' become its own component or stay as a roadmap variant? Watch for a third doc that wants the same pattern before extracting.

agent_decisions.this_render_history: Second render of the doc-explained source. The first render (2026-05-14T11:44:28Z) used only the starter visual vocabulary — Interactive 4, Computational 1. User caught the narrowing: 'this section seems to be narrowing the possibilities of how the doc can be structured.' Course correction: refined premise 4 from 'Warm AND computational, no tradeoff' to 'Interactive, Computational, Aesthetic, and Warm' (four co-equal pillars). Rewrote SKILL.md to make computational behaviors first-class and 'invent' the default. Re-rendered this doc with parseCsv, renderBarChart, clickToExplain, and glossary primitives baked into runtime.js.
agent_decisions.v1_extraction_candidates: After two docs, three reuse signals: (a) the flow component appears in both docs and is the visual workhorse; (b) the callout component (clay + sage) appears in both; (c) the clickToExplain harness — newly invented here — generalizes the flow-node pattern and is the strongest V1 behavior candidate. Recommend extracting flow + clickToExplain together when a third doc lands.
agent_decisions.notes: All four pillars hit. Self-audit recorded above. Brand mark held at 'HD' for cross-doc visual cohesion. Glossary scope-skips topbar, nav, headings, code, and meta blocks to avoid noisy highlighting.
